summarization.a: The plot shows the variation of etch rate with modification step duration for all studied materials at a constant 10 s activation step. At short modification times, the etch rate is very low due to incomplete surface modification. TiN exhibits nearly zero etch rate, while SiO₂ shows a higher rate due to argon sputtering and does not exhibit a clear ALE window. For Al₂O₃, AlNₓ, and HfO₂, the etch rate increases with modification duration and approaches saturation, indicating the onset of self-limiting behavior.
summarization.b: The enlarged plot focuses on Al₂O₃, AlNₓ, and HfO₂, highlighting the ALE window between 5 s and 12 s of modification. In this region, the etch rate reaches a stable plateau, confirming controlled layer-by-layer removal. At longer modification times, the etch rate slightly decreases due to excessive fluorocarbon film thickness limiting surface activation during ion bombardment.

data_extraction.a:
| Material | Modification Step Duration (s) | Etch Rate (nm/cycle) | Observation |
|---|---|---|---|
| TiN | 0–14 | ~0.00 | Etch rate remains near zero; no ALE behavior |
| SiO₂ | 0 | 0.05 | Slight sputtering observed at zero modification |
| SiO₂ | 10–12 | 0.25–0.30 | Slight plateau; no clear ALE window |
| Al₂O₃ | 0–14 | 0.05→0.35 | Rate increases then saturates |
| AlNₓ | 0–14 | 0.05→0.32 | Similar trend to Al₂O₃ |
| HfO₂ | 0–14 | 0.05→0.30 | Saturation behavior observed |
data_extraction.b:
| Material | Modification Step Duration (s) | Etch Rate (nm/cycle) | Observation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Al₂O₃ | 0 | 0.05 | Low initial etch rate |
| Al₂O₃ | 5–12 | 0.25–0.35 | ALE window observed; rate saturates |
| Al₂O₃ | 14 | 0.20 | Slight decline due to thick film formation |
| AlNₓ | 0 | 0.05 | Low initial rate |
| AlNₓ | 5–12 | 0.20–0.30 | ALE window region |
| AlNₓ | 14 | 0.18 | Reduced rate after saturation |
| HfO₂ | 0 | 0.05 | Low etch rate |
| HfO₂ | 5–12 | 0.20–0.28 | Stable ALE window |
| HfO₂ | 14 | 0.15 | Decrease after prolonged modification |

d.question: What material-dependent behaviors distinguish ALE-active materials from non-ALE materials in the figure?
d.answer: Presence of an etch-rate saturation plateau with increasing modification time, Formation of a clear ALE window at intermediate modification durations, Reduced sensitivity to argon sputtering effects, Ability to form a self-limiting modified surface layer

c.question: Which material shows nearly zero etch rate across all modification step durations?

a.question: Do Al₂O₃, AlNₓ, and HfO₂ exhibit a stable ALE window at intermediate modification step durations?

b.question: How does increasing the modification step duration influence the etch rate during the ALE process for oxide and nitride materials?
b.answer: At short modification durations, the surface is only partially covered by the fluorocarbon film, resulting in very low etch rates due to insufficient surface activation. As the modification time increases, a more complete reactive layer forms, enabling efficient ion-assisted reactions during the activation step and causing the etch rate to rise. Beyond an optimal duration, the etch rate saturates, reflecting self-limiting behavior, and may slightly decrease at longer times due to excessive film thickness hindering ion penetration and surface reaction efficiency.
